# Literature Review

This chapter surveys the academic foundations relevant to reinforcement learning
for high-frequency trading. It is organized in three parts. The first part
reviews market microstructure — the empirical evidence and theoretical
mechanisms that govern short-horizon price formation in limit order book markets.
This establishes the financial environment in which the agent operates and
motivates the feature engineering choices made in Chapter 5. The second part
positions reinforcement learning as the appropriate methodological framework for
this problem and surveys its application in trading research. The third part
synthesizes the literature to identify the gap this thesis addresses.
